comment: Tests short-lived CQ behavior: (1) CQ is one-shot — queue is empty after tick, beacon re-enqueues on next cycle; (2) beacon off stops CQ immediately, no stale CQ accumulates; (3) beacon on resumes CQ; (4) active QSO preempts CQ — CQ sorts below QSO entries; (5) after QSO completes or exhausts, CQ resumes.

periods.comment: Period 0 (even): beacon on → first CQ. Queue should be empty after tick (one-shot).
Period 1 (odd): no TX (odd slot). Queue should be empty (CQ was popped by tick).
Period 2 (even): beacon still on → fresh CQ (proves first was popped, not retried).
Period 3 (odd): beacon OFF. No stale CQ should remain.
Period 4 (even): no CQ (beacon off). Queue empty.
Period 6 (even): beacon ON again → CQ resumes.
Period 7 (odd): DX replies with TX1 (grid). CQ was popped by tick; DX creates a new QSO context via on_decode → override. Now active QSO should preempt any future CQ.
Period 8 (even): should TX to DX (TX2 report), NOT CQ. QSO preempts beacon.
Period 9 (odd): DX sends TX3 (R+report). QSO advances to ROGERS, logs ADIF.
Period 10 (even): should TX RR73 to DX (ROGERS state), not CQ.
Period 11 (odd): DX sends TX5 (73). QSO complete → IDLE → pop. Queue empty.
Period 12 (even): QSO done, beacon still on → CQ resumes.
